subject: First Year Algebra
instructions: Answer 4 questions. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problems.text: Prove that the following polynomials are irreducible in \(\mathbb{Q}[x]\), stating clearly any theorems you wish to apply.
Let \(R\) be the quotient of the ring \(\mathbb{Z}[i]\) of Gaussian integers by the principal ideal \((3+3i)\). Show that \(R\) is finite and determine the number of its elements.
Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with 1 and let \(I\), \(J\) and \(P\) be ideals in \(R\) such that \(I\cap J\subseteq P\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(P\) is a prime ideal then either \(I\subseteq P\) or \(J\subseteq P\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example which shows that if \(P\) is not prime then the conclusion above may not hold.

problems.text: Let \(R\) be a ring with 1 and \(M\) a (unital) left \(R\)-module.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: State what it means for \(M\) to satisfy the Ascending Chain Condition (i.e. for \(M\) to be Noetherian).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(M\) satisfies the ACC then every submodule of \(M\) is finitely generated.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give an example of a finitely generated module that does not satisfy the ACC.

problems.text: Determine the conjugacy classes of elements of order 8 in \(\mathrm{GL}(4,\mathbb{Q}(\sqrt{2}))\). (Hint: Factorize \(x^4+1\) in \(\mathbb{Q}(\sqrt{2})[x]\).)
Let \(E\) be an extension field of the field \(F\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: What does it mean for \(E\) to be an algebraic extension of \(F\)?

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that if \(\alpha\) and \(\beta\) are elements of \(E\) that are both roots of the same irreducible polynomial in \(F[x]\), then the subfields \(F(\alpha)\) and \(F(\beta)\) of \(E\) are isomorphic.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give an example where the subfields in (b) are not equal.
